title: IOM Uplink Utilization
exclusions: Northbound Fabric Interconnect to Cisco Nexus (or other upstream) LAG state and `^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-` scoping: UC-19.1.5. Per-FI-port CRC, FCS, and discard counter velocity on `sys/switch-…/slot-…/…-port-…`: UC-19.1.13. IOM card thermal, voltage, and fan health via `equipmentIOCardStats` and like classes: UC-19.1.6. X-series IFM (Intelligent Fabric Module) paths use the same chassis/slot/fabric/port dn tree as B-series IOMs: in scope, but you must have accurate model to link-speed mapping. Standalone C-series on CIMC without UCSM does not offer this MO shape: out of scope. Cisco HyperFlex with UCS Manager statistics may partially overlap, but the authoritative support channel is the HX manager and storage SLOs: treat as out of scope for first-line triage. Intersight Managed Mode domains that do not deliver classic cisco:ucs:stats: pair with an Intersight telemetry strategy (sibling UC-19.1.22 for the Intersight anchor in this category) rather than an empty index.
dataSources: `index=cisco_ucs` and sourcetype `cisco:ucs:stats` from the Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS. Include `classId` values `etherIomStats`, `etherIomTxStats`, `etherIomRxStats`, and `fabricEthLanEpStats` in the **Cisco UCS statistics modular input** `stats_classes` so the poller fetches the right managed objects. The on-wire utilization SPL in this use case only evaluates the `etherIom*` IOM port classes. `fabricEthLanEpStats` is optional (same DNs, fabric view) for validation. The IOM–FI path is the dn tree `sys/chassis-*/slot-*/fabric-(a|b)/port-*` (IOM in slot, port index per IOM model). Cumulative `totalBytesRx` / `totalBytesTx` (or direction-split analogues) are monotonically increasing until a clear, flap, or reboot. The search converts 5m deltas to bps, then to util_pct against link bps (operSpeed in Mbps with coalesce, plus `ucs_iom_uplink_speed` lookup) with coalesce for field-name drift.
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` (Splunkbase 2731; install on a Heavy Forwarder or a host that can reach UCSM with outbound HTTPS).
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-4h
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| where cidl in ("etheriomstats","etheriomtxstats","etheriomrxstats")
| rex field=dn "^(?i)sys/chassis-(?<chassis_num>\d+)/slot-(?<iom_slot>\d+)/fabric-(?<fabric>[ab])/port-(?<port_num>\d+)$"
| where isnotnull(chassis_num)
| eval tbr=tonumber(coalesce(totalBytesRx, total_bytes_rx, totalbytesrx, bytesRx, bytes_rx, "0"))
| eval tbt=tonumber(coalesce(totalBytesTx, total_bytes_tx, totalbytestx, bytesTx, bytes_tx, "0"))
| eval part_bytes=case(cidl=="etheriomstats",tbr+tbt,cidl=="etheriomtxstats",tbt,cidl=="etheriomrxstats",tbr,1=1,null())
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(eval(if(cidl=="etheriomstats",tbr+tbt,null()))) as b_agg, sum(eval(if(cidl=="etheriomtxstats" OR cidl=="etheriomrxstats",if(isnull(part_bytes),0,part_bytes),0))) as b_split, last(operSpeed) as operSpeed_l, last(oper_speed) as oper_sp_l, last(model) as mod_f, last(mdl) as mdl_f by _time, dn, chassis_num, iom_slot, fabric, port_num
| eval bytes_total=if(isnotnull(b_agg),b_agg,b_split)
| eval operSpeed=coalesce(operSpeed_l,oper_sp_l)
| eval iom_model=coalesce(mod_f,mdl_f,"DEFAULT_IOM_10G")
| fields - b_agg, b_split, operSpeed_l, oper_sp_l, mod_f, mdl_f
| sort 0 dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(bytes_total) as prev_bytes by dn
| eval delta_bytes=if(isnull(prev_bytes) OR isnull(bytes_total),0,if(bytes_total>=prev_bytes,bytes_total-prev_bytes,bytes_total))
| eval bin_seconds=300
| eval bps=if(bin_seconds>0,delta_bytes/bin_seconds,0)
| eval operSpeed_mbps=tonumber(operSpeed)
| lookup ucs_iom_uplink_speed iom_model OUTPUT port_speed_bps as lookup_bps
| eval link_speed_bps=coalesce(tonumber(operSpeed_mbps)*1000000,if(tonumber(lookup_bps)>0,tonumber(lookup_bps),null()),10000000000)
| eval util_pct=round(tonumber(bps)*8/link_speed_bps*100,1)
| sort 0 dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(util_pct) as prior1 by dn
| streamstats current=f last(prior1) as prior2 by dn
| eval severity_bucket=case(util_pct>=90 AND prior1>=90 AND prior2>=90,"critical",(util_pct>=90 OR prior1>=90) OR (util_pct>=75 AND prior1>=75),"warning",util_pct>=60,"watch",1=1,"ok")
| eventstats max(util_pct) as chassis_peak by chassis_num,_time
| table _time,chassis_num,iom_slot,fabric,port_num,util_pct,bps,operSpeed_mbps,link_speed_bps,severity_bucket,chassis_peak,dn,iom_model
| sort 0 - _time, -util_pct
description: Per-IOM-to-FI uplink capacity: this use case ingests IOM port statistics that Cisco UCS Manager exposes under the `etherIomStats`, `etherIomTxStats`, and `etherIomRxStats` `classId` values in `cisco:ucs:stats`, applies a dn filter `^sys/chassis-\\d+/slot-\\d+/fabric-(a|b)/port-\\d+$` so each row is a single IOM (or the same path shape for an X-series IFM) uplink to a parent Fabric Interconnect, and turns cumulative byte counters into 5m deltas with an explicit if(bytes_total<prev_bytes) reset branch so clear counters, link flaps, and poll gaps do not emit negative bps. Link capacity uses coalesce(operSpeed in megabits per second times 10^6, lookup bps, 10G default) so the percentage reflects physical line rate. Sibling UCs: UC-19.1.5 reports northbound LAG to Nexus health on the `^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-` tree. UC-19.1.13 reports per-FI port CRC and error velocity on the `sys/switch-…/…-port-…` tree. This is the third axis: IOM-fabric headroom in percent-of-capacity on the chassis IOM or IFM to FI path for east–west and storage-heavy congestion before upstream LAG symptoms appear.
value: A hot IOM uplink toward the Fabric Interconnect throttles every blade and VM that share that IOM’s uplink budget, so latency, migration stalls, and storage-path congestion can show up with UC-19.1.5 still green and UC-19.1.13 still at baseline error rates. The business outcome is earlier capacity and placement decisions (add an uplink, re-home a vNIC, or rebalance DRS) before a chassis-wide service risk. A second outcome is a clean hand-off: operations can label short PXE, backup, or batch spikes as “expected” using change records, while separate sustained 90% bins still route to a capacity queue rather than a silent wire fault queue.
implementation: Install Splunkbase 2731, enable the **Cisco UCS statistics modular input** with `enable_stats` and a `stats_classes` list that includes at least `etherIomStats`, `etherIomTxStats`, `etherIomRxStats`, `fabricEthLanEpStats`, and `etherPIo` (for `operSpeed` when the TA places it on that class). Add `lookups/ucs_iom_uplink_speed.csv` and a `transforms.conf` entry. Run the `spl` in Search, save as a scheduled report with 5m cadence, and route alert actions by the `severity_bucket` with throttle keys `chassis_num, iom_slot, fabric, port_num`.
visualization: Heatmap: chassis_num × (iom_slot, fabric, port) cell color = util_pct (1h). Table: 95th percentile util_pct, link_speed_bps, operSpeed_mbps, severity_bucket. Line: util_pct 24h by fabric. Single value: count of rows with severity in warning or critical. Optional: timechart of chassis_peak to highlight worst chassis per time bucket.

references.title: Cisco UCS Manager XML API Reference (classes including etherIomStats)
Cisco UCS Manager 4.x Statistics Management Guide

knownFalsePositives: Short-lived 100% during PXE or kickstart for a planned bare-metal build if that chassis is the install target. vMotion and live-migration surges when DRS rebalances a cluster. Rolling UCSM or IOM firmware where one fabric reloads and traffic pins to the surviving path so utilisation is high by design. Deliberate single-fabric maintenance where all vNICs fail over to the other fabric and that fabric runs near ceiling until the window closes. Veeam, Commvault, or Avamar backup and replication windows that max egress to IP storage. End-of-quarter report runs on large databases. Planned Nexus vPC or Direct Connect lab failover where saturation is the test objective. vDC or workload evacuation jobs that pack east-west on one pair of uplinks. Short Hadoop or Spark shuffle phases you already accept. Chassis you intentionally run hot for NetApp NFS or Pure Block patterns with written SLO approval.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites and three-axis placement (read before install)

This use case is the IOM-fabric capacity axis in the three-axis Cisco UCS network health model used alongside UC-19.1.5 and UC-19.1.13. UC-19.1.5 answers whether the northbound LAN port-channel to the upstream Nexus (or other upstream) is logically healthy on the `^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-` tree. UC-19.1.13 answers whether a specific Fabric Interconnect Ethernet leg is accreting CRC- and error-class counter velocity on the `sys/switch-…/…-port-…` tree. This UC-19.1.16 answers a third question: on the `sys/chassis-N/slot-M/fabric-(a|b)/port-K` tree, is each IOM (or X-series IFM) uplink toward the parent Fabric Interconnect running out of capacity headroom? All three can show green, yellow, and red independently; do not treat one green as proof for the other two. You need Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs (Splunkbase 2731) on a host that can reach the UCSM XML API, an index (here `cisco_ucs`) that ingests cisco:ucs:stats, and a statistics modular input that actually polls etherIomStats. Prove ingestion with: index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-1h | stats count by classId. If classId=etherIomStats never appears, stop and fix the input before you tune SPL.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Create a read-only service account in UCSM (Admin, User Management): example `splunk_stats_reader` with read-only or equivalent read-only style role. On Splunk, open **Settings → Data inputs** and locate the **Cisco UCS statistics modular input** (label varies by 2731 minor release) so you can set xml_url, credentials, polling interval, and the MO class list. Alternatively author `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/local/inputs.conf` and merge, never hand-edit `default/`.

```ini
# Example ciscoucs stanza; align keys to your 2731 build and store secrets in the credential store or UI, not a repo.
[ciscoucs://prod_ucsm]
index = cisco_ucs
disabled = 0
xml_url = https://ucsm.example.com/nuova
username = splunk_stats_reader
password = <set via secret storage, not clear text>
polling_interval = 300
verify_ssl = true
enable_stats = true
stats_classes = etherIomStats,etherIomTxStats,etherIomRxStats,fabricEthLanEpStats,etherPIo
# fabricEthLanEpStats: optional validation double-check; etherPIo: operSpeed for link width when present
```

UCSM typically presents TLS with a self-signed or enterprise CA certificate; import the full CA chain into the Splunk host trust store, or in lab only use verify_ssl false until the CA is trusted. The statistics feed uses **cisco:ucs:stats**; there is no supported `cisco:ucs:iom_stats` string on 2731—if an internal wiki still uses that name, update the wiki to prevent empty searches. After two polling cycles, run index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-1h | stats count by classId and expect etherIomStats when stats_classes is wide enough. First managed-object class in focus for bytes: **etherIomStats** (aggregate Rx/Tx byte counters for the IOM port). The split classes **etherIomTxStats** and **etherIomRxStats** provide direction-split byte counters; the sample SPL coalesces so you prefer the aggregate when present and only sum the split path when the aggregate row is absent. **fabricEthLanEpStats** is listed in the modular input for optional FI-side cross-validation (it is a fabric endpoint view) but is not in the main SPL, so you do not double-count. Physical link speed: prefer operSpeed in megabits per second on the same poll line when the TA provides it, else join a parent **etherPIo** row, else fall back to a small lookup. Place `lookups/ucs_iom_uplink_speed.csv` in your app, wire transforms.conf, and use the lookup in the `spl` field.

UTF-8 CSV (no BOM) seed with eight rows; extend with the exact `model` strings you see in equipmentIOCard in your org:

```csv
iom_model,port_speed_bps,notes
UCSB-IOM-2204XP,10000000000,4×10G to FI when ports map 1:1 at 10G
UCSB-IOM-2208XP,10000000000,8×10G uplink map with common FI designs
N20-I6625-001,10000000000,legacy SKU you may see in older inventories
UCSB-IOM-2408,25000000000,25G-class uplink when licensed and cabled
UCSB-IOM-2304,40000000000,4×40G to FI
UCSX-I-9108-25G,25000000000,IFM 25G per Cisco BOM
UCSX-I-9108-100G,100000000000,IFM 100G per Cisco BOM
DEFAULT_IOM_10G,10000000000,coalesce key when model is missing; tighten after you harvest inventory
```

`transforms.conf` companion stanzas:

```ini
[ucs_iom_uplink_speed]
filename = ucs_iom_uplink_speed.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

Paste the SPL block below into Search; it is the only supported primary pipeline for the detection math.

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-4h
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| where cidl in ("etheriomstats","etheriomtxstats","etheriomrxstats")
| rex field=dn "^(?i)sys/chassis-(?<chassis_num>\d+)/slot-(?<iom_slot>\d+)/fabric-(?<fabric>[ab])/port-(?<port_num>\d+)$"
| where isnotnull(chassis_num)
| eval tbr=tonumber(coalesce(totalBytesRx, total_bytes_rx, totalbytesrx, bytesRx, bytes_rx, "0"))
| eval tbt=tonumber(coalesce(totalBytesTx, total_bytes_tx, totalbytestx, bytesTx, bytes_tx, "0"))
| eval part_bytes=case(cidl=="etheriomstats",tbr+tbt,cidl=="etheriomtxstats",tbt,cidl=="etheriomrxstats",tbr,1=1,null())
| bin _time span=5m
| stats max(eval(if(cidl=="etheriomstats",tbr+tbt,null()))) as b_agg, sum(eval(if(cidl=="etheriomtxstats" OR cidl=="etheriomrxstats",if(isnull(part_bytes),0,part_bytes),0))) as b_split, last(operSpeed) as operSpeed_l, last(oper_speed) as oper_sp_l, last(model) as mod_f, last(mdl) as mdl_f by _time, dn, chassis_num, iom_slot, fabric, port_num
| eval bytes_total=if(isnotnull(b_agg),b_agg,b_split)
| eval operSpeed=coalesce(operSpeed_l,oper_sp_l)
| eval iom_model=coalesce(mod_f,mdl_f,"DEFAULT_IOM_10G")
| fields - b_agg, b_split, operSpeed_l, oper_sp_l, mod_f, mdl_f
| sort 0 dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(bytes_total) as prev_bytes by dn
| eval delta_bytes=if(isnull(prev_bytes) OR isnull(bytes_total),0,if(bytes_total>=prev_bytes,bytes_total-prev_bytes,bytes_total))
| eval bin_seconds=300
| eval bps=if(bin_seconds>0,delta_bytes/bin_seconds,0)
| eval operSpeed_mbps=tonumber(operSpeed)
| lookup ucs_iom_uplink_speed iom_model OUTPUT port_speed_bps as lookup_bps
| eval link_speed_bps=coalesce(tonumber(operSpeed_mbps)*1000000,if(tonumber(lookup_bps)>0,tonumber(lookup_bps),null()),10000000000)
| eval util_pct=round(tonumber(bps)*8/link_speed_bps*100,1)
| sort 0 dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(util_pct) as prior1 by dn
| streamstats current=f last(prior1) as prior2 by dn
| eval severity_bucket=case(util_pct>=90 AND prior1>=90 AND prior2>=90,"critical",(util_pct>=90 OR prior1>=90) OR (util_pct>=75 AND prior1>=75),"warning",util_pct>=60,"watch",1=1,"ok")
| eventstats max(util_pct) as chassis_peak by chassis_num,_time
| table _time,chassis_num,iom_slot,fabric,port_num,util_pct,bps,operSpeed_mbps,link_speed_bps,severity_bucket,chassis_peak,dn,iom_model
| sort 0 - _time, -util_pct
```

The pipeline does five things. First, it normalizes `classId` with lower(coalesce(…)) and filters the three etherIom* IOM port classes, then it rex-filters the dn to the chassis/slot/fabric/port tree for IOM-to-FI links. Second, it coalesce-normalizes `totalBytesRx` / `totalBytesTx` (and per-direction fallbacks) so minor TA renames do not break the math. Third, it bins to five minutes and, per bin, prefers b_agg from etherIomStats when that row exists; if not, it sums the per-direction part_bytes on the split classes so you are not double-counting aggregate and split in the same window. Fourth, it sorts by dn and time, then uses streamstats last(bytes_total) as prev_bytes and if(bytes_total>=prev_bytes, bytes_total-prev_bytes, bytes_total) to turn monotonic byte totals into a delta, treating a counter clear or flap as a new epoch. Fifth, it converts bytes per 300s into bps, multiplies by eight for line-rate bits, divides by **link_speed_bps** (operSpeed in Mbps times one million, then coalesce with lookup bps, then 10G default) to form util_pct, then applies a second streamstats pair to carry prior1 and prior2 for severity logic: critical is three consecutive bins with util at or above 90% on that dn; warning covers a single 90% bin or two consecutive 75% bins, or a single 90% on a prior cell per the case statement; watch is util_pct at or above 60% in the current bin. It finishes with eventstats max(util_pct) as chassis_peak by chassis and _time for dashboard roll-up. In saved-search form, set earliest to -2h to -4h, schedule on */5, and add throttle keys chassis_num, iom_slot, fabric, port_num. Map Major at roughly 85% to warning in your ticketing tool and 70% to watch in chat when you use static thresholds; align the numeric mapping to the severity_bucket column your board displays.

**Step 3 — Validate**

(a) On the Nexus (or other upstream) that faces the Fabric Interconnect, use your port matrix to find the CLI interface that corresponds to a hot IOM in Splunk, then run `show interface` style counters in the same 15 to 20 minute window and compare order-of-magnitude bps, allowing one to two minutes of API skew versus UCSM polling. (b) In the UCSM GUI, Equipment, Chassis, IOM, Fabric port statistics for the same port and compare 5–15 minute averages to util_pct, not a single instantaneous poll. (c) In Splunk: index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" classId=etherIomStats | head 5 | table dn, classId, totalBytesRx, totalBytesTx, operSpeed to sample fields. (d) In a maintenance window, run an iperf3 or storage-heavy job between blades to push east-west traffic; you should see bps and util_pct rise, then fall when the test ends. (e) | inputlookup ucs_iom_uplink_speed must return rows; if it errors, the transform path or file permissions on the search head are wrong. (f) | timechart max(chassis_peak) by chassis_num in a test search lets you see whether one chassis dominates. (g) If you can execute a safe clear counters on a test FI or UCSM for one interface with a change record, the delta should not dip negative: the if(bytes_total<prev_bytes) path takes bytes_total as the fresh increment base.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

Dashboard: heatmap of chassis by port using util from the last hour; table of the top chassis_num, iom_slot, fabric, port_num by 95th percentile util_pct; line chart 24h util_pct split by fabric a vs b; single value of count of links in warning or critical in the last hour. Saved search: cron */5 * * * * with a window covering at least two 300s polls, alert when severity_bucket is warning or critical, throttle on chassis and port dimensions. Webhook: include chassis, slot, fabric, port, util_pct, chassis_peak, a deep link to your UCSM equipment page if you have one, and a link to a saved Splunk search with the same earliest window. Runbook: owner (compute and network), RACI for IOM and FI optics versus Nexus-side port policy, and when to open Cisco TAC with FRU and serial data.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**

(1) Zero results: you are in the wrong index, sourcetype is not cisco:ucs:stats, or stats_classes in inputs.conf does not list etherIomStats. Run index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" | stats count by classId, index, sourcetype and read the class list. (2) util_pct absurd (negative, thousands of percent): streamstats reset missing, or a dedup before streamstats, or you summed cumulative totals without the delta. Re-paste the provided spl. (3) operSpeed null on many lines: add a join or subsearch that pulls classId=etherPIo for the same port dn parent and merge operSpeed, or lean on the lookup and inventory rows. Filter adminState on etherPIo if you add that join, so you only compute util on up ports. (4) operSpeed 25000 in UCSM but 10G at the SFP: speed mismatch in optics or transceiver map; file a configuration follow-up, not a blind capacity ticket. (5) You see 2x util: both etherIomStats and split rows present in a rare build; the pipeline prefers b_agg; if your site still double-counts, add a per-lab | dedup dn on raw events with care. (6) IFM in X series: the dn still matches; ensure your iom_model string in events matches a lookup row. (7) Very large scale: pre-aggregate to summary index with 5m buckets, never store raw twice. (8) Noisy watch tier on expected Hadoop or backup floors: add a second saved search with a 2-sigma or percentile deviation per chassis, not a single static 60% everywhere. (9) UCS domains that only have Intersight and no classic UCSM stats for those FIs: this UC is out of scope; use Intersight telemetry and UC-19.1.22 class content as your anchor, not an empty cisco:ucs:stats index as proof of health.

Closing: keep UC-19.1.5, UC-19.1.13, and UC-19.1.16 on one operational dashboard. Bundle health, error velocity, and IOM-fabric headroom are three lenses on related hardware; the lenses do not replace each other.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the small switch card in each server chassis that funnels all blade traffic up to the big fabric switches. If that pipe gets crowded, every virtual machine in the box can slow down at once, even when other network checks look fine. We help you see the crowded pipes early so you can add capacity or move workloads before people call the help desk.
hardware: Cisco UCS IOM (I/O Module) — IOM 2204XP / 2208XP / 2304 / 2408 in B-series chassis (UCSB-5108), and IFM (Intelligent Fabric Module) UCSX-I-9108-25G / UCSX-I-9108-100G in X-series chassis (UCSX-9508). Each IOM provides 4-8 southbound 10G/25G/40G/100G uplinks to a parent Fabric Interconnect (UCS 6300/6400/6500 series).
